Animated Armor (BB).name: Animated Armor (BB)
Animated Armor (BB).acDetails: (13 after construct armor breaks), construct armor
Animated Armor (BB).publicNotes:
Objects can be given a semblance of life through the use of rituals or other strange magic, turning them into animated objects. These objects move, work, and fight based on the commands given to them by their creators, who usually set them on guard duty or have them perform menial labor.

Suits of animated armor might serve as guardians, but sometimes high-end martial academies use them as training partners. They're most often found in wizards' laboratories and ancient dungeons.

action->Construct Armor.name: Construct Armor
action->Construct Armor.description: The animated armor reduces all damage it takes by 9. However, after a critical hit that deals damage, or after its HP drops below 10, its construct armor breaks. Its AC drops to 13 and it no longer reduces damage by 9.

Basilisk (BB).name: Basilisk (BB)
Basilisk (BB).publicNotes: A basilisk is a reptile that can turn creatures to stone just by looking at them.

action->Petrifying Glance.name: Petrifying Glance
action->Petrifying Glance.description:
When a creature the basilisk can see within 30 feet starts its turn, the basilisk can use its reaction to glance at the creature.

The creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:20|options:inflicts:slowed] save. If it fails, it gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} condition for 1 minute as its body slowly stiffens.

action->Petrifying Gaze.name: Petrifying Gaze
action->Petrifying Gaze.description:
The basilisk stares at a creature it can see within 30 feet. If the creature can see the basilisk, that creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:22|options:inflicts:slowed,inflicts:petrified] save. If it fails and hasn't already gained the slowed condition from Petrifying Glance or this ability, it gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} condition. If the creature fails this save when it already has the slowed condition from this ability or Petrifying Glance, the creature is permanently turned to stone.

A creature petrified in this manner that is coated with fresh basilisk blood is instantly restored to flesh. A single basilisk contains enough blood to coat up to 2 creatures in this manner.

Boar (BB).name: Boar (BB)
Boar (BB).publicNotes: Foul-tempered and tough, boars are far more dangerous than domesticated pigs. These omnivorous mammals are hunted heavily because their meat is considered a delicacy. Boars are most likely to attack humanoids in self-defense or during their mating season in the winter months, when the males grow an extra inch of tissue to protect their organs as they fight off rivals. In some cultures, boars are trained to become much more aggressive for use as war beasts and guardians. When such boars escape back into the wild, they can become true terrors of the region.

action->Ferocity.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.N1kstYbHScxgUQtN]

When the boar is reduced to 0 Hit Points, it can use its reaction to remain conscious with 1 Hit Point, but it gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Yl48xTdMh3aeQYL2]{Wounded 1} condition (or increases its wounded value by 1 if it already has that condition). It can't use this ability at wounded 3.

action->Boar Charge.name: Boar Charge
action->Boar Charge.description: The boar Strides twice and then makes a tusk Strike. As long as it moved at least 20 feet, it gains a +2 circumstance bonus to its attack roll.

Brine Shark (BB).name: Salzhai (EB)
Brine Shark (BB).publicNotes:
Wasserelementare durchstreifen die endlosen Ozeane der Ebene des Wassers. Sie können beschworen werden, wechseln zuweilen aber auch in die normale Welt über, wenn Naturkatastrophen wie Überschwemmungen oder Orkane eintreten. Wasserelementare haben großes Zerstörungspotential, sind aber nicht immer bösartig. So wie Wasser den Bedürftigen Leben bringen kann, können die Wellen auch gegen das Ufer brechen und der Regen Städte überschwemmen. Wasserelementare sind ähnlich unberechenbar.

Die kampffreudigen Salzhaie zählen zu den schwächeren Wasserelementare und ähneln komplett aus Wasser bestehenden Haien. Oft wechseln sie auch in Ozeane des Universums über und messen sich mit den dortigen Raubtieren oder schließen sich sogar Schulen normalsterblicher Haie an.

action->Deep Plunge.name: Tieftauchen
action->Deep Plunge.description: Der Salzhai taucht in gerader vertikal Linie ins Wasser hinab und bewegt sich dabei um bis zu 100 Fuß/30 m weit. Er kann diese Fähigkeit nutzen, während er eine Kreatur @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Gegriffen} hat.

action->Grab.description: Gelingt dem Salzhai ein Bissangriff, kann er sofort diese Aktion nutzen und versuchen das Ziel zu @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.PMbdMWc2QroouFGD]{Ergreifen}. Dieser Versuch unterliegt nicht dem Malus für Mehrfachangriffe und wirkt sich auch nicht auf diesen aus.

Bugbear Prowler (BB).name: Bugbear Prowler (BB)
Bugbear Prowler (BB).publicNotes: These stealthy and cruel creatures delight in spreading fear and tormenting their victims. Bugbears are the monsters lurking in the closet and hiding under the bed. Preying on remote farmsteads, bugbears reveal their presence with thumps in the night or creaking boards.

action->Bushwhack.name: Bushwhack
action->Bushwhack.description: The bugbear prowler Strides up to 10 feet and attempts to [[/act grapple]] a creature that can't see them and doesn't know where they are. If the bugbear succeeds, they also deal damage from their fist Strike to that creature.

action->Mauler.name: Mauler
action->Mauler.description: The bugbear prowler gains a +3 circumstance bonus to damage rolls against creatures they have @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}.

Bugbear Tormentor (BB).name: Bugbear Tormentor (BB)
Bugbear Tormentor (BB).publicNotes: The bugbear tormentor seeks to bully their prey as much through psychological intimidation as through physical harm.

action->Sneak Attack.name: Sneak Attack
action->Sneak Attack.description: The bugbear tormentor's Strikes deal 1d6 extra precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures.

action->Twin Feint.name: Twin Feint
action->Twin Feint.description: The bugbear tormentor makes a dazzling series of attacks with two weapons, using the first attack to throw their foe @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} against a second attack at a different angle. They make one Strike with each of their two melee weapons, both against the same target. The target is automatically off-guard against the second attack. The bugbear tormentor applies their multiple attack penalty to these Strikes normally.

Caligni Dancer (BB).name: Calignitänzer (EB)
Caligni Dancer (BB).publicNotes: Die Calignis hausen in unterirdischen Städten, und sie genießen die Gelegenheit, nachts an die Oberfläche zu kriechen, um Ressourcen zu stehlen, Oberflächenbewohner zu beschatten oder einfach nur Unfug zu treiben.

action->Light Blindness.name: Blindheit durch Licht
action->Light Blindness.description: Wenn der Caligni erstmalig einem Hellen Licht ausgesetzt wird, kann er bis zum Ende seines nächsten Zuges nichts sehen. Alle Felder sind für ihn Schwieriges Gelände und er erleidet einen Zustandsmalus von -4 auf Wahrnehmung. Versucht er eine Kreatur anzugreifen, dann schlägt der Angriff fehl, sofern er nicht einen erfolgreichen @Check[flat|dc:11]{Einfachen Wurf} ablegt. Danach blendet das Licht das Monster für 1 Stunde nicht mehr.

action->Final Dance.name: Letzter Tanz
action->Final Dance.description: Wenn ein Caligni-Tänzer stirbt, löst sich sein Körper in einem Wirbel aus Dunkelheit und Licht auf. Alle Kreaturen innerhalb von @Template[emanation|distance:10]{10 Fuß/3 m} müssen einen @Check[will|dc:17]{Willenswurf} bestehen oder sie erleiden für 1 Runde einen Situationsmalus von -2 auf alle Angriffswürfe.

action->Sneak Attack.description: Der Caligni-Tänzer fügt Kreaturen zusätzliche 1W6 Schaden zu, sollten sie den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Auf dem Falschen Fuß} besitzen.

Caligni Hunter (BB).name: Caligni-Jäger (EB)
Caligni Hunter (BB).publicNotes: Die Calignis hausen in unterirdischen Städten, und sie genießen die Gelegenheit, nachts an die Oberfläche zu kriechen, um Ressourcen zu stehlen, Oberflächenbewohner zu beschatten oder einfach nur Unfug zu treiben.

action->Light Blindness.name: Blindheit durch Licht
action->Light Blindness.description: Wenn der Caligni erstmalig einem Hellen Licht ausgesetzt wird, kann er bis zum Ende seines nächsten Zuges nichts sehen. Alle Felder sind für ihn Schwieriges Gelände und er erleidet einen Zustandsmalus von -4 auf Wahrnehmung. Versucht er eine Kreatur anzugreifen, dann schlägt der Angriff fehl, sofern er nicht einen erfolgreichen @Check[flat|dc:11]{Einfachen Wurf} ablegt. Danach blendet das Licht das Monster für 1 Stunde nicht mehr.

action->Sneak Attack.name: <Compendium>
action->Sneak Attack.description: Der Caligni-Jäger fügt Kreaturen zusätzliche 1W6 Schaden zu, sollten sie den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Auf dem Falschen Fuß} besitzen.

action->Final Fate.name: Letztes Schicksal
action->Final Fate.description: Wenn ein Caligni-Jäger stirbt, explodiert sein Körper. Alle Kreaturen innerhalb von @Template[emanation|distance:20]{20 Fuß/6 m} erleiden @Damage[5d6[fire]|options:area-damage]{5W6 Feuerschaden}. Jeder Kreatur muss einen @Check[reflex|dc:19|basic|options:area-effect]{einfachen Willenswurf} ablegen.

action->Darkening Poison.name: Verdunklungsgift
action->Darkening Poison.description: Wenn der Jäger das nächste Mal mit einem Angriff seines Kurzschwerts Schaden zufügt, muss das Ziel einen @Check[fortitude|dc:16|traits:damaging-effect]{Zähigkeitswurf} ablegen oder es erleidet @Damage[1d8[poison]]{1W8 Punkte Giftschaden} und behandelt für 1 Runde alle Kreaturen als @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Verborgen}.

Caligni Skulker (BB).name: Caligni-Schleicher (EB)
Caligni Skulker (BB).publicNotes: Die Calignis hausen in unterirdischen Städten, und sie genießen die Gelegenheit, nachts an die Oberfläche zu kriechen, um Ressourcen zu stehlen, Oberflächenbewohner zu beschatten oder einfach nur Unfug zu treiben.

action->Light Blindness.name: Blindheit durch Licht
action->Light Blindness.description: Wenn der Caligni erstmalig einem Hellen Licht ausgesetzt wird, kann er bis zum Ende seines nächsten Zuges nichts sehen. Alle Felder sind für ihn Schwieriges Gelände und er erleidet einen Zustandsmalus von -4 auf Wahrnehmung. Versucht er eine Kreatur anzugreifen, dann schlägt der Angriff fehl, sofern er nicht einen erfolgreichen @Check[flat|dc:11]{Einfachen Wurf} ablegt. Danach blendet das Licht das Monster für 1 Stunde nicht mehr.

action->Final Night.name: Letzte Nacht
action->Final Night.description: Wenn ein Caligni-Schleicher stirbt, löst sich sein Körper in einen Nebel tiefster Dunkelheit auf. Alle Lichtquellen innerhalb von @Template[emanation|distance:20]{20 Fuß/6 m} werden ausgelöscht. Alle Licht-Zauber im Wirkungsbereich erlöschen für 1 Runde.

action->Darkening Poison.name: Verdunklungsgift
action->Darkening Poison.description: Wenn der Schleicher das nächste Mal mit einem Angriff seines Dolchs Schaden zufügt, muss das Ziel einen @Check[fortitude|dc:16|traits:damaging-effect]{Zähigkeitswurf} ablegen oder es erleidet @Damage[1d8[poison]]{1W8 Punkte Giftschaden} und behandelt für 1 Runde alle Kreaturen als @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Verborgen}.

action->Sneak Attack.name: <Compendium>
action->Sneak Attack.description: Der Caligni-Schleicher fügt Kreaturen zusätzliche 1W6 Schaden zu, sollten sie den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Auf dem Falschen Fuß} besitzen.

Central Spears (BB).name: Speerfalle (EB)
Central Spears (BB).disable: Ein Held muss einen Fertigkeitswurf für @Check[thievery|dc:18|traits:hazard|name:Mechanismus ausschalten]{Diebeskunst} bei den auslösenden Bodenplatten oder den Wandöffnungen in der Westwand ablegen.
Central Spears (BB).reset: Die Speerfalle setzt sich nach 1 Minute zurück.

action->Spear Barrage.name: Speerhagel
action->Spear Barrage.description: Wenn eine Kreatur eines der Felder im markierten Bereich betritt, schießen drei Speere aus der Westwand. Die Falle führt einen Fernkampfangriff gegen die westlichste Kreatur in jeder Reihe aus.

Cinder Rat (BB).name: Funkenratte (EB)
Cinder Rat (BB).publicNotes:
Feuerelementare sind destruktive Manifestationen von der Ebene des Feuers. Manchmal wechseln sie in die normale Welt an Orten großer Hitze und Flammen über, wo elementare Wirbel entstehen - z.B. in Vulkanen.

Funkenratten sind übergroße Nagetiere aus rauchender Holzkohle und elementarem Feuer. Aus ihm flammenden Fleisch quillen beständig giftige Dämpfe. Wie normale Ratten suchen sie sich abgeschiedene Bauten und können sich unkontrolliert schnell vermehren.

action->Smoke Vision.name: Rauchsicht
action->Smoke Vision.description: Die Funkenratte ignoriert den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Verborgen}, so er aus Rauch resultiert.

action->Fetid Fumes.name: Stinkende Dämpfe
action->Fetid Fumes.description: Die Funkenratte ist von einer Rauchwolke umgeben. Alle Kreaturen innerhalb eines @Template[emanation|distance:5]{5 Fuß/1,50 m-Radius} (die Funkenratte eingeschlossen) erhalten den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Verborgen} durch diesen Rauch. Betritt eine Kreatur die Wolke oder beginnt sie darin ihren Zug, muss sie einen @Check[fortitude|dc:22]{Zähigkeitswurf} ablegen; bei Misslingen erleidet sie 1 Runde lang einen Zustandsmalus von -1 auf alle Würfe und SG (Nutze hierfür den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Kränkelnd 1}).

Crawling Hand (BB).name: Crawling Hand (BB)
Crawling Hand (BB).publicNotes: Crawling hands form when severed appendages are endowed with a crude sentience by necromantic energies that turn them into tireless killers.

action->Lifesense.name: Lifesense
action->Lifesense.description: A crawling hand can sense the vital essence of living and undead creatures within 30 feet. This sense can distinguish between the vitality energy animating living creatures and the void energy animating undead creatures, much as sight distinguishes color. However, a crawling hand can't see, so effects that depend on vision or seeing don't work on it.

action->Throat Grab.name: Throat Grab
action->Throat Grab.description: When the crawling hand succeeds at its claw Strike against a Medium or smaller creature, it can use this action immediately after to attempt to [[/act grapple]] the target. This Grapple doesn't apply or count toward the crawling hand's multiple attack penalty. A creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} this way has difficulty speaking and must spend an extra action to perform any action that requires speaking, including casting spells.

Doppelganger (BB).name: Doppelgänger (EB)
Doppelganger (BB).publicNotes: Doppelgänger können sich in perfekte Kopien anderer Kreaturen verwandeln, während ihre natürlichen Gestalten im Grunde undefinierbar sind.

action->End the Charade.name: Ende der Scharade
action->End the Charade.description:
Hat der Doppelgänger mittels Gestalt wechseln eine andere Gestalt angenommen und bewegt sich eine andere Kreatur auf ein angrenzendes Feld oder führt sie eine feindselige Handlung gegen ihn aus, so kann er diese Reaktion nutzen. Der Doppelgänger nimmt dann seine natürliche Gestalt an und kann einen Angriff gegen die auslösende Kreatur ausführen.

War dieser Kreatur nicht bewusst, dass der Doppelgänger sich verkleidet hatte, dann besitzt sie gegen diesen Angriff den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Auf dem Falschen Fuß}.

action->Change Shape.description: Der Doppelgänger nimmt die Gestalt eines bestimmten Kleinen oder Mittelgroßen Humanoiden an, den er gesehen hat. Dies ändert nichts an der Bewegungsrate sowie den Angriffs- und Schadensboni seiner Angriffe, kann aber die Schadensart seiner Angriffe ändern (für gewöhnlich zu Wuchtschaden).

action->Mind Reading.name: Oberflächliche Gedanken lesen
action->Mind Reading.description: Der Doppelgänger versucht, die oberflächlichen Gedanken einer maximal 30 Fuß/9 m weit entfernten Kreatur zu lesen. Dem Ziel steht hiergegen ein @Check[will|dc:21]{Willenswurf} zu.

spell->Magic Weapon.name: <Compendium>
spell->Magic Weapon.target: 1 Waffe, die entweder nicht getragen oder von dir oder einem bereitwilligen Verbündeten geführt wird

shield->Shield.name: Schild
shield->Shield.description: Ein Schild hat 20 Trefferpunkte. Wenn ein Schild jemals 10 Schaden oder mehr erleidet, erhält er den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6dNUvdb1dhToNDj3]{Beschädigt}. Ein beschädigter Schild kann nicht mehr genutzt werden, um @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.xjGwis0uaC2305pm]{Schild heben} oder @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.jM72TjJ965jocBV8]{Schildblock} auszuführen, du kannst ihn aber @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.bT3skovyLUtP22ME]{Reparieren}. Wenn der Schild alle 20 Trefferpunkte verliert, wird er komplett zerstört - dann kann er auch nicht mehr repariert werden.

action->Light Blindness.description: Wenn der Dunkelelf hellem Licht ausgesetzt wird, kann er vorübergehend @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{nichts sehen}; er erhält bis zum Ende seines nächsten Zuges einen Zustandsmalus von -4 auf Wahrnehmungswürfe und alle Felder zählen für ihn als Schwieriges Gelände. Er muss ferner einen @Check[flat|dc:11]{Einfachen Wurf} ablegen, wenn er eine Kreatur angreifen will; bei Misslingen scheitert der Angriff automatisch. Im Anschluss ist er für 1 Stunde gegen diese Schwäche immun.

action->Shield Block.description: Hat die Priesterin seit ihrem letzten Zug einen @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.xjGwis0uaC2305pm]{Schild gehoben}, kann sie ihre Reaktion nutzen, um einen Angriff zu blocken. Reduziere den Schaden des Angriffes um 5. Sowohl die Priesterin wie auch ihr Schild erleiden den restlichen Schaden. Dies kann dem Schild den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6dNUvdb1dhToNDj3]{Beschädigt} oder sogar Zerstört verleihen.

Drow Sneak (BB).name: Dunkelelfenschurke (EB)
Drow Sneak (BB).publicNotes: Dunkelelfen sind im Grunde Einzelgänger und trauen niemandem - oder zumindest keinem anderen Dunkelelfen. Diese Dunkelelfen verlassen sich auf ihre Wachsamkeit und Anpassungsfähigkeit, um in ihrer gnadenlos-halsabschneiderischen Gesellschaft zu überleben.

action->Light Blindness.description: Wenn der Dunkelelf hellem Licht ausgesetzt wird, kann er vorübergehend @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{nichts sehen}; er erhält bis zum Ende seines nächsten Zuges einen Zustandsmalus von -4 auf Wahrnehmungswürfe und alle Felder zählen für ihn als Schwieriges Gelände. Er muss ferner einen @Check[flat|dc:11]{Einfachen Wurf} ablegen, wenn er eine Kreatur angreifen will; bei Misslingen scheitert der Angriff automatisch. Im Anschluss ist er für 1 Stunde gegen diese Schwäche immun.

action->Quick Draw.name: Schnelle Waffenbereitschaft
action->Quick Draw.description: Der Dunkelelfenschurke benutzt die Aktion Interagieren, um eine Waffe zu ziehen und sofort mit ihr anzugreifen.

action->Sneak Attack.description: Der Dunkelelfenschurke verursacht bei Kreaturen, die ihm gegenüber @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Auf dem Falschen Fuß} betroffen sind, zusätzlich 1W6 Punkte Schaden.

Drow Warrior (BB).name: Dunkelelfenkrieger (EB)
Drow Warrior (BB).publicNotes: Dunkelelfen sind die Nachkommen von Elfen, welche vor langer Zeit in tiefe unterirdische Höhlen geflohen sind und dort von einem großen Bösen verderbt wurden.

action->Light Blindness.description: Wenn der Dunkelelf hellem Licht ausgesetzt wird, kann er vorübergehend @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{nichts sehen}; er erhält bis zum Ende seines nächsten Zuges einen Zustandsmalus von -4 auf Wahrnehmungswürfe und alle Felder zählen für ihn als Schwieriges Gelände. Er muss ferner einen @Check[flat|dc:11]{Einfachen Wurf} ablegen, wenn er eine Kreatur angreifen will; bei Misslingen scheitert der Angriff automatisch. Im Anschluss ist er für 1 Stunde gegen diese Schwäche immun.

action->Quick Draw.name: Schnelle Waffenbereitschaft
action->Quick Draw.description: Der Dunkelelfenkämpfer benutzt die Aktion Interagieren, um eine Waffe zu ziehen und sofort mit ihr anzugreifen.

action->Skewer.name: Durchbohren
action->Skewer.description: Der Dunkelelfenkämpfer greift mit seinem Rapier an und erhält einen Situationsbonus von +1 auf seinen Angriffswurf. Eine von diesem Angriff verletzte Kreatur erleidet @Damage[1d6[bleed]]{1W6 Punkte Blutungsschaden}.

Envenomed Lock (BB).name: Envenomed Lock (BB)
Envenomed Lock (BB).description: A spring-loaded, poisoned spine is hidden near the keyhole of a lock. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.cYdz2grcOcRt4jk6]{Disabling} or breaking the trap doesn't disable or break the lock.
Envenomed Lock (BB).disable: @Check[thievery|dc:17|traits:hazard|name:Disable the Spring Mechanism] on the spring mechanism

action->Spring.name: Spring
action->Spring.description: When a creature tries to unlock or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.2EE4aF4SZpYf0R6H]{Pick the Lock}, a spine extends to attack that creature with a Spine attack.

Falling Ceiling (BB).name: Herabstürzende Steinblöcke (EB)
Falling Ceiling (BB).disable: Ein Held muss einen erfolgreichen Fertigkeitswurf für @Check[thievery|dc:18|traits:hazard|name:Steinblöcke am Fallen hindern]{Diebeskunst} am Feld mit dem Auslöser oder der Decke ablegen, um die Steinblöcke am Herabstürzen zu hindern.

action->Falling Stones.name: Herabstürzende Steine
action->Falling Stones.description: Tritt eine Kreatur auf das Feld vor der Nordtür, stürzen im ganzen Gang Steine herab. Die Falle führt einen Fernkampfangriff gegen jede Kreatur zwischen den beiden Türen.

Falling Shelves.name: Falling Shelves
Falling Shelves.disable: A hero must succeed at a @Check[athletics|dc:18] or @Check[thievery|dc:18] check to prevent the shelves from collapsing.

action->Topple Over.name: Topple Over
action->Topple Over.description: When a creature attempts to touch the shelves or take anything from them, they collapse, sending debris throughout the room. The trap makes one melee Strike against each creature in the room.

Ferrous Butterfly (BB).name: Ferrous Butterfly (BB)
Ferrous Butterfly (BB).publicNotes: Beautiful but deadly, the ferrous butterfly is a turkey-sized insectile creature bristling with dozens of gleaming, razor-edged blades. Considered harmless by most denizens of the Plane of Metal, they nonetheless pose a significant threat to organic creatures, whose iron-rich blood they find irresistible. The wing blades of these butterflies tend to break off during feeding and regrow naturally. These scales are reminiscent of jagged scraps of metal and can serve as surprisingly effective makeshift weapon.

action->A Thousand Cuts.name: A Thousand Cuts
action->A Thousand Cuts.description:
A ferrous butterfly's knifelike wings cause terrible lacerations that continue to bleed. Any creature with persistent bleed damage from a ferrous butterfly's wing attack has weakness 2 to slashing damage until the bleeding is stopped.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Eecbbol29eIP3zvf]{Effect: A Thousand Cuts}

action->Swoop.name: Swoop
action->Swoop.description: The ferrous butterfly Flies up to its Speed and makes a wing Strike at any point during that movement.

Forest Troll (BB).name: Forest Troll (BB)
Forest Troll (BB).publicNotes: Forest trolls are dim-witted, gangly giants who stalk the fringes of civilization. They rely on their incredible strength to overpower foes with their vicious claws. A troll's endless hunger drives them to consume a variety of living creatures. Trolls stand anywhere from 12 to 16 feet tall, though they prefer to hunch to lull foes into a false sense of security.

action->Regeneration 20 (Deactivated by.name:
<Compendium> tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.lR9R5Vld8Eu2Dha5]
Regeneration 20 (Deactivated by Electricity or Fire)
action->Regeneration 20 (Deactivated by.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.lR9R5Vld8Eu2Dha5]

The troll regains 20 Hit Points at the start of their turn unless they've taken electricity or fire damage since their last turn. The troll can't die unless their regeneration is deactivated when they're reduced to 0 HP.

action->Easily Misled.name: Easily Misled
action->Easily Misled.description: The forest troll gets a –4 circumstance penalty to their Perception DC against deception checks.

action->Rend.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.3JOi2cMcGhT3eze1]

If the troll succeeds at two claw Strikes against the same creature in the same turn, they can use this ability to automatically deal claw Strike damage to that creature.

Gargoyle (BB).name: Gargoyle (BB)
Gargoyle (BB).publicNotes: Gargoyles are monstrous hunters made entirely of stone. They look like decorative statues and use this as camouflage to hide in plain sight in cities during the day and descend upon unlucky pedestrians at night. Their most common form is that of a horned humanoid with batlike wings, but individual gargoyles vary greatly, with some appearing more or less humanoid and others resembling something else entirely. Gargoyles tend to be lone hunters, though sometimes they band into groups called wings.

action->Clawed Feet.name: Clawed Feet
action->Clawed Feet.description: If the gargoyle is flying and a creature moves into an adjacent square below them, the gargoyle can use their reaction to make a claw Strike against that creature.

action->Statue.name: Statue
action->Statue.description: Until the next time they use an action, the gargoyle appears to be a statue. A creature must succeed at a @Check[perception|dc:32] check to figure out that the statue is really a creature.

action->Site Bound.name:
<Compendium> tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-family-ability-glossary.Item.BcSlVpaN72LoQ5BV]
Site Bound
action->Site Bound.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-family-ability-glossary.Item.BcSlVpaN72LoQ5BV]

A ghost can stray no more than 120 feet from where it was killed or the place it haunts. Some are bound to items or creatures that were special to them rather than a location.

action->Incorporeal.name: Incorporeal
action->Incorporeal.description: A ghost has no body and can pass through physical objects, though it can't end its turn inside one. Most checks that use Strength and require a body, such as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.PMbdMWc2QroouFGD]{Grapple}, don't work against a ghost, and a ghost can't attempt such checks.

action->Rejuvenation.name:
<Compendium> tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-family-ability-glossary.Item.unR8VVR4yyRnsmnB]
Rejuvenation
action->Rejuvenation.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-family-ability-glossary.Item.unR8VVR4yyRnsmnB]

Setting right the injustice that led to the ghost's death allows it to move on to the afterlife. Otherwise, when destroyed, the ghost re-forms after [[/gmr 2d4 #days]]{2d4 days} within the location it's bound to, fully healed.

action->Frightful Moan.name:
<Compendium> tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-family-ability-glossary.Item.aiwlPSmNY9b6Psvd]
Frightful Moan
action->Frightful Moan.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-family-ability-glossary.Item.aiwlPSmNY9b6Psvd]

Each living creature within @Template[emanation|distance:30]{30 feet} who hears the ghost must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21|options:area-effect,inflicts:frightened] save. On a failure, a creature gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} condition (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3} on a critical failure).

On a success, a creature can't be affected by this ghost's Frightful Moan for 1 minute.

Ghoul Stalker (BB).name: Ghoul Stalker (BB)
Ghoul Stalker (BB).publicNotes: The flesh-eating undead, known as ghouls, haunt sinister locations such as lonely graveyards and ruined crypts, making meals of corpses. Legends say that those captured by ghouls hear foul whispers that curse them into craving raw meat, eventually turning them into ghouls.

action->Stench.name: Stench
action->Stench.description: A creature that comes within 10 feet of the ghoul and can smell the ghoul must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:14|options:area-effect,inflicts:sickened] save. On a failure, the creature takes a –1 status penalty to all checks and DCs until it uses an action to retch (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}). A creature that succeeds at its save isn't affected by any ghoul's stench for 1 minute.

action->Consume Flesh.name:
<Compendium> tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-family-ability-glossary.Item.ixPqVlqLaYTB1b23]
Consume Flesh
action->Consume Flesh.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-family-ability-glossary.Item.ixPqVlqLaYTB1b23]

The ghoul devours a chunk of a creature that died within the last hour and regains @Damage[1d6[healing]] Hit Points.

The ghoul can regain Hit Points from a given body only once.

action->Sneak Attack.name: Sneak Attack
action->Sneak Attack.description: The ghoul stalker deals an extra 1d4 damage to creatures that have the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} condition.

action->Swift Leap.name:
<Compendium> tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-family-ability-glossary.Item.UU1Fp3PRuTFONjC9]
Swift Leap

Giant Ant (BB).name: Giant Ant (BB)
Giant Ant (BB).publicNotes: Giant ants form vast underground colonies, excavating deep burrows or infesting existing caverns. Giant ants look identical to their smaller kin, but their size makes them a deadly threat to farms and travelers alike.

action->Giant Ant Venom.name: Giant Ant Venom
action->Giant Ant Venom.description: A target that takes piercing damage from the giant ant's stinger Strike must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:18|options:damaging-effect] save or take @Damage[1d10[poison]] damage.

action->Haul Away.name: Haul Away
action->Haul Away.description: If the giant ant has a creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} with its mandibles, it can spend this action to Stride up to its speed, carrying the creature with it. At the end of the Stride, place the creature in any open square adjacent to the giant ant.

action->Grab.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.Tkd8sH4pwFIPzqTr]

When the ant succeeds at its mandibles Strike, it can use this action immediately after to attempt to [[/act grapple]] the target. This Grapple doesn't apply or count toward the ant's multiple attack penalty.

Giant Centipede (BB).name: Giant Centipede (BB)
Giant Centipede (BB).publicNotes:
Centipedes are relatively common arthropods that live, hunt, and scavenge amid dung and detritus. Scurrying about with surprising speed on the scores of legs attached to their long, segmented bodies, centipedes strike with poisoned mandibles.

Most giant centipedes (known as sewer centipedes when found in cities) nest in small groups but hunt alone. Attempts to domesticate giant centipedes for use as guardians or pets generally end poorly, but some goblin and kobold societies have developed effective methods of utilizing these vermin as guardians. Some societies roast and eat centipedes, often with pungent peppers, as a savory delicacy. Care must be taken in preparing the meal, though, to avoid contaminating the flesh with the creature's venom.

action->Giant Centipede Venom.name: Giant Centipede Venom
action->Giant Centipede Venom.description: A target that takes piercing damage from the centipede's mandibles Strike must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:14|options:damaging-effect] save or take @Damage[1d6[poison]] damage.

Giant Fly (BB).name: Giant Fly (BB)
Giant Fly (BB).publicNotes: This massive fly's twitching limbs seem to never stop moving as it constantly cleans itself and scoops anything edible into its mouth parts. While primarily carrion eaters, giant flies sometimes prey on livestock or other animals too slow to get away. Left to their own devices, these flies will lay dozens of eggs within the mostly-eaten carcasses. These eggs soon hatch into enormous maggots that begin the cycle of scavenging anew.

action->Avoid the Swat.name: Avoid the Swat
action->Avoid the Swat.description:
Trigger The giant fly is targeted with a melee or ranged attack by an attacker it can see

Effect The giant fly gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If the attack misses, the giant insect can Fly up to its fly Speed.

action->Fly Pox.name: Fly Pox
action->Fly Pox.description: A giant fly could carry any disease, but most transmit an infection called fly pox with their bite. A character who is critically hit by a giant fly's mandibles must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:16|options:inflicts:enfeebled] saving throw or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 day.

Giant Rat (BB).name: Giant Rat (BB)
Giant Rat (BB).publicNotes:
Giant rats are enormous versions of the common vermin. They're typically found in abundant numbers, but since they can't fit in the nooks where ordinary rats typically hide, they're much easier to locate and exterminate. In fact, finding and slaying a giant rat is a rite of passage for many an adventurer. Such tasks get tiresome quickly, so many veterans subcontract extermination to new adventurers.

Giant rats mostly live in sewers where they can scavenge from the streets above, but some families of giant rats live in more remote locations, such as dank caves, forests, or hills. Rats are incredibly adept survivors and can be found nearly anywhere in the world, though they tend to favor temperate or warm climates as opposed to cold regions.

Although their bite alone isn't lethal except to the very weak, some giant rats carry a dangerous disease common to rodents around the world—a pestilence more than capable of ravaging rural communities. The rats are merely carriers with no risk of succumbing to the disease themselves.

Giant Spider (BB).name: Giant Spider (BB)
Giant Spider (BB).publicNotes: Few everyday vermin inspire as much dread as the infamous spider. Giant spiders vary in size. The human-sized versions, sometimes called hunting spiders, are the most common type of giant spider, though not the largest.

action->Descend on a Web.name: Descend on a Web
action->Descend on a Web.description: The giant spider moves straight down up to 40 feet, suspended by a web line. It can hang from the web or drop off. The distance it Descends on a Web doesn't count for falling damage. A creature can Strike the web, which has AC 20 and 8 HP. If the web is reduced to 0 HP, the spider falls.

action->Giant Spider Venom.name: Giant Spider Venom
action->Giant Spider Venom.description: A target damaged by the spider's fangs must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:16|options:damaging-effect,inflicts:off-guard] save or take @Damage[1d10[poison]] damage and gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} condition for 1 round.

action->Web Trap.name: Web Trap
action->Web Trap.description: A creature hit by the giant spider's web Strike gets stuck to the nearest surface. It can't move until it successfully [[/act escape dc=17]]{Escapes}.

Giant Viper (BB).name: Giant Viper (BB)
Giant Viper (BB).publicNotes:
Snakes come in an array of forms, from jungle-dwelling constrictors that wrap around their prey to venomous vipers with deadly bites. Regardless, all snakes consume prey whole by stretching their lower jaw and using powerful muscles to move the food down their throats and into their stomachs.

The giant viper's fangs, nearly as long as daggers, easily inspire fear. The venom injected by a giant viper can cause severe blood clotting and inflict tremendous damage.

action->Coiled Opportunity.name:
<Compendium> tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.hFtNbo1LKYCoDy2O]
Coiled Opportunity
action->Coiled Opportunity.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.hFtNbo1LKYCoDy2O]

If the giant viper is Coiled and a creature moves into or through a square adjacent to it, the giant viper can use its reaction to make a fangs Strike against that creature.

action->Coil.name: Coil
action->Coil.description: The giant viper uses an action to coil itself. While coiled, it can target a creature up to 10 feet away with its fangs Strike. After the giant viper Strikes with its fangs, it becomes uncoiled

action->Giant Viper Venom.name: Giant Viper Venom
action->Giant Viper Venom.description: A target damaged by the snake's fangs must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:17|options:damaging-effect] save or take @Damage[1d8[poison]] damage.

Goblin Commando (BB).name: Goblin Commando (BB)
Goblin Commando (BB).publicNotes:
Most goblin communities teeter in a state of barely controlled disarray—a collective of creatures operating largely on gut instinct rarely follows any clear direction. Even goblin leaders tend to be impulsive, irresponsible, and generally just not that good at actually leading.

In theory, goblin commandos lead goblin raids. In practice, goblin commandos rarely continue to lead their comrades once a battle has begun. Most shirk their responsibilities in favor of wading into the fray and claiming glory from their kin.

action->Goblin Scuttle.name: Goblin Scuttle
action->Goblin Scuttle.description: If a goblin ally ends a move action adjacent to the goblin commando, the commando can use their reaction to Step.

Goblin Igniter (BB).name: Goblin Igniter (BB)
Goblin Igniter (BB).publicNotes:
Goblins find fire totally fascinating. It makes light. It's great for cooking. It's a fun toy! Some goblins take their people's admiration of fire a little bit too far and into the realm of deadly obsession.

Goblins who turn their attention to magic so they can learn to make fire from nothing are known as goblin igniters. These flame fanatics can be a great boon to a band of goblin raiders eager to torch their enemies and wreak havoc. More often, however, their presence is a double-edged sword. In the heat of the moment, goblin igniters sometimes lose sight of their goals and simply set fire to anything that will burn—including their own allies!

action->Goblin Scuttle.name: Goblin Scuttle
action->Goblin Scuttle.description: If a goblin ally ends a move action adjacent to the goblin igniter, the igniter can use their reaction to Step.

Goblin Warrior (BB).name: Goblin Warrior (BB)
Goblin Warrior (BB).publicNotes:
The frontline fighters of goblin tribes prefer to fight in large groups—especially when they can outnumber their foes by at least three to one. One doesn't need intense training or great toughness to become a goblin warrior. Anyone can aspire to this role, or be drafted into it during an emergency.

Goblin warriors delight in wreaking havoc. They think nothing of slaughtering livestock, stealing infants, or burning down buildings purely for momentary delight. They revel in playing malicious tricks on taller humanoids, whom they call "longshanks." What the goblins lack in consistency, long-term planning, and good sense, they make up for in sheer bravery and commitment.

action->Goblin Scuttle.name: Goblin Scuttle
action->Goblin Scuttle.description: If a goblin ally ends a move action adjacent to the goblin warrior, the warrior can use their reaction to Step.

Harpy (BB).name: Harpy (BB)
Harpy (BB).publicNotes: Harpies are an amalgamation of human and bird, using their ancestral control of the wind to lure prey or even directly pull in their next meal. Harpies can eat most creatures, but prefer to eat humans and elves in particular. Harpies live in family groups or larger clans, although they rarely hunt in big groups, preferring to keep most meals for themselves.

action->Stench.name: Stench
action->Stench.description: A creature that comes within 30 feet of the harpy and can smell the harpy must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:21|options:area-effect,inflicts:sickened] save. On a failure, the creature takes a –1 status penalty to all checks and DCs until it uses an action to retch (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}). A creature that succeeds at its save isn't affected by any harpy's stenches for 1 minute.

action->Hungry Winds.name: Hungry Winds
action->Hungry Winds.description: The harpy uses the wind to pull its prey closer. A target within 20 feet must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:21|options:forced-movement] save or be pulled adjacent to the harpy. The harpy then makes a jaws strike against the target. If the target is pulled into the air and cannot fly, it falls as normal.

Hell Hound (BB).name: Hell Hound (BB)
Hell Hound (BB).publicNotes: Temperamental and quick to aggressive behavior, hell hounds are fiendish canines from a hellish plane. They're skilled hunters and can breathe gouts of flame from their maws. A hell hound's appearance leaves no doubts as to its infernal origins—flesh the color of burning pitch, teeth as sharp as any fiend's pitchfork, and a shroud of ever-burning hellfire are all trademark features. Though they can't speak, they understand Diabolic, the language of Hell.

action->Hellfire Breath.name: Hellfire Breath
action->Hellfire Breath.description:
The hell hound breathes flames that deal @Damage[4d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage to all creatures in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15]. Each affected creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:19|basic|options:area-effect] save.

The hell hound can't use Hellfire Breath again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Hellfire Breath]]{1d4 rounds}. If the hell hound would take fire damage or is the target of a fire effect, its Hellfire Breath recharges.

action->Pack Attack.name: Pack Attack
action->Pack Attack.description: The hell hound's jaws Strike deals 1d4 extra damage to creatures within reach of two of the hell hounds' allies.

Hidden Pit (BB).name: Hidden Pit (BB)
Hidden Pit (BB).description: A wooden trapdoor covers a 10-foot square pit that's 20 feet deep.
Hidden Pit (BB).disable: @Check[thievery|dc:12|traits:hazard|name:Remove the Trapdoor] to remove the trapdoor.
Hidden Pit (BB).stealth: (or @Check[perception|dc:0|traits:hazard] if the trapdoor is disabled or broken)

action->Pitfall.name: Pitfall
action->Pitfall.description: If a creature walks onto the trap door, that creature falls in unless it succeeds at a [[/act grab-an-edge dc=16]]{Reflex} save to grab hold of the edge. The pit is 20 feet deep, dealing @Damage[10[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage to anyone who falls to the bottom.

Hobgoblin Archer (BB).name: Hobgoblin Archer (BB)
Hobgoblin Archer (BB).publicNotes: Bands of hobgoblin soldiers typically have at least one archer among their ranks. In smaller groups, the hobgoblin archer also serves as that band's captain. These archers generally command these groups from a distance or higher ground and they make use of their keen senses to watch for strategic openings.

action->Formation.name: Formation
action->Formation.description: When they're adjacent to at least two other allies, the hobgoblin archer gains a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and saving throws.

action->Crossbow Precision.name: Crossbow Precision
action->Crossbow Precision.description: The first time the archer hits with a crossbow attack in a round, they deal 1d8 extra precision damage.

action->Running Reload.name: Running Reload
action->Running Reload.description: The archer Strides, Steps, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.VMozDqMMuK5kpoX4]{Sneaks}, then Interacts to reload.

Hobgoblin Soldier (BB).name: Hobgoblin Soldier (BB)
Hobgoblin Soldier (BB).publicNotes: Hobgoblins are devoted to war, and their entire civilization is built upon fostering and maintaining conflict while proving their battle superiority. Highly organized, they work efficiently in groups, whether that group is a small raiding party, a roving war band, or a fully regimented army. Physically, hobgoblins stand about as tall as humans and have gray, ashen skin.

shield->Shield.name:
<Compendium> tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Yr9yCuJiAlFh3QEB]
Shield

action->Formation.name: Formation
action->Formation.description: When they're adjacent to at least two other allies, the hobgoblin soldier gains a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and saving throws.

action->Raise a Shield.name: Raise a Shield
action->Raise a Shield.description: The hobgoblin soldier lifts up their shield to get a +2 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of their next turn

action->Shield Block.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.m4HQ2o5aPxjXf2kY]

If they @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.xjGwis0uaC2305pm]{Raised a Shield} since their last turn, the hobgoblin can spend their reaction to block a Strike. Reduce the damage by 5, but the hobgoblin and the shield both take the remaining damage, possibly breaking or destroying the shield.

action->Twisting Tail.name: Twisting Tail
action->Twisting Tail.description: When a creature within 10 feet uses a move action or leaves a square during a move action it's using, the dragon can use their reaction to make a tail Strike at the creature with a –2 penalty. If it hits, the creature stops moving in the square it was in when the dragon used this reaction.

action->Draconic Frenzy.name: Draconic Frenzy
action->Draconic Frenzy.description: The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.

action->Draconic Momentum.name: Draconic Momentum
action->Draconic Momentum.description: When the dragon gets a critical success on a Strike, they recharge their Poison Breath.

action->Poison Breath.name: Poison Breath
action->Poison Breath.description: The dragon breathes a toxic cloud that deals @Damage[5d6[poison]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:30]. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save. The dragon can't use Poison Breath again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Poison Breath]]{1d4 rounds}.

action->Sneak Attack.description: Zolgran fügt Kreaturen, welche den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Auf dem Falschen Fuß} besitzen, zusätzliche 1W6 Schadenspunkte zu.

Kobold Mage (Black, BB).name: Koboldmagier (Schwarz, EB)
Kobold Mage (Black, BB).publicNotes: Kobolde sind kleine, reptilische Humanoide, die häufig im Dienst mächtigerer Kreaturen stehen. Sie lauern an dunklen Orten, meistens Tunnel und Minen. Koboldzauberer nutzen Magie für ihre Pläne, meist im Dienst eines Drachen oder einer anderen mächtigen Kreatur.

action->Scamper.name: Weghuschen
action->Scamper.description: Befindet sich der Kobold zu wenigstens einem Gegner angrenzend, läuft er bis zu 30 Fuß/9 m weit und erhält er einen Situationsbonus von +2 auf seine RK gegen Reaktionen, die er durch diese Bewegung auslöst. Er darf seine Bewegung nicht auf einem zu einem Gegner angrenzenden Feld beenden.

Kobold Mage (Blue, BB).name: Koboldmagier (Blau, EB)
Kobold Mage (Blue, BB).publicNotes: Kobolde sind kleine, reptilische Humanoide, die häufig im Dienst mächtigerer Kreaturen stehen. Sie lauern an dunklen Orten, meistens Tunnel und Minen. Koboldzauberer nutzen Magie für ihre Pläne, meist im Dienst eines Drachen oder einer anderen mächtigen Kreatur.

action->Scamper.description: Befindet sich der Kobold zu wenigstens einem Gegner angrenzend, läuft er bis zu 30 Fuß/9 m weit und erhält er einen Situationsbonus von +2 auf seine RK gegen Reaktionen, die er durch diese Bewegung auslöst. Kobold Mage (Red, BB).publicNotes: Kobolde sind kleine, reptilische Humanoide, die häufig im Dienst mächtigerer Kreaturen stehen. Sie lauern an dunklen Orten, meistens Tunnel und Minen. Koboldzauberer nutzen Magie für ihre Pläne, meist im Dienst eines Drachen oder einer anderen mächtigen Kreatur.

Kobold Mage (White, BB).name: Koboldmagier (Weiß, EB)
Kobold Mage (White, BB).publicNotes: Kobolde sind kleine, reptilische Humanoide, die häufig im Dienst mächtigerer Kreaturen stehen. Sie lauern an dunklen Orten, meistens Tunnel und Minen. Koboldzauberer nutzen Magie für ihre Pläne, meist im Dienst eines Drachen oder einer anderen mächtigen Kreatur.
